title: Choose a distribution path for a new open-source CLI
decisionStatement: Which release and installation strategy should a small maintainer team adopt for the first six months of a new TypeScript CLI?
context: The CLI has a public repository, deterministic tests, versioned GitHub release artifacts, and a small early-user group. Its command-line and schema contracts may still change. Maintainers want low-friction installation without creating support obligations they cannot sustain.

inScope: GitHub release artifacts, npm registry publication, and source installation
Versioning, checksums, upgrade instructions, and rollback
Maintainer and user support cost during public preview
outOfScope: Operating-system-specific package managers in the first release
constraints: Every published artifact must be reproducibly inspectable
Breaking preview changes must be documented
The team can spend at most two maintainer-hours per week on distribution support

criteria.label: Installation success
criteria.description: How reliably a new user reaches a working CLI from clean instructions.
criteria.zeroAnchor: Most users cannot install without maintainer help
criteria.oneAnchor: At least eighty percent install unaided on supported systems

criteria.label: Maintainer load
criteria.description: The recurring operational and support burden created by the distribution path.
criteria.zeroAnchor: The release channel exceeds the support budget
criteria.oneAnchor: Routine releases and support fit comfortably within the budget

criteria.label: Supply-chain clarity
criteria.description: How easily users can identify, verify, and reproduce the intended artifact.
criteria.zeroAnchor: Artifact origin and integrity are ambiguous
criteria.oneAnchor: Origin, contents, version, and checksum are explicit

criteria.label: Release reversibility
criteria.description: How safely maintainers and users can recover from a bad release.
criteria.zeroAnchor: A bad release has no practical rollback path
criteria.oneAnchor: Previous versions remain installable and rollback is documented
